name: Army Publishing Directorate (FM / ADP / ATP / TC)
note: Authoritative source for all Army Field Manuals, Doctrine Pubs, Techniques Pubs, and Training Circulars (incl. public weapons training circulars e.g. TC 3-22.9 Rifle & Carbine). Publisher: Army Publishing Directorate.

name: Combined Arms Research Library (CARL) Digital Library
note: Open digital library of Army doctrine, history, and reference holdings (Command & General Staff College). Publisher: Army University Press.

name: Joint Doctrine Publications (JP series)
note: Official Joint Publications — JP 1, JP 3-0 Joint Operations, JP 5-0 Joint Planning, etc. Publisher: Joint Chiefs of Staff.

name: Joint Electronic Library (JEL)
note: Full joint doctrine library and doctrine notes. Publisher: Joint Chiefs of Staff.

name: Marine Corps Pubs Electronic Library (MCDP / MCRP / MCTP)
note: Marine Corps Doctrinal, Reference, and Tactical Publications — incl. MCDP 1 Warfighting. Publisher: U.S. Marine Corps.

name: Air Force e-Publishing (AFDP / AFI / AFMAN)
note: Official Air Force doctrine, instructions, and manuals. Publisher: U.S. Air Force.

name: Navy Directives & Instructions (DONI)
note: Official Navy directives, instructions, and notices. Publisher: Dept. of the Navy Issuances (DONI).

name: NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 — Security & Privacy Controls
note: Catalog of security and privacy controls for federal information systems. Publisher: NIST CSRC.

name: NIST SP 800-37 Rev 2 — Risk Management Framework
note: RMF for information systems and organizations — the core RMF process. Publisher: NIST CSRC.

name: NIST SP 800-171 Rev 2 — Protecting CUI
note: Protecting Controlled Unclassified Information in nonfederal systems (DFARS / contractor relevant). Publisher: NIST CSRC.

name: NIST SP 800-171 Rev 3 — Protecting CUI
note: Latest revision of CUI protection requirements. Publisher: NIST CSRC.

name: NIST SP 800-207 — Zero Trust Architecture
note: Foundational zero-trust architecture reference. Publisher: NIST CSRC.

name: NIST SP 800-61 Rev 2 — Incident Handling Guide
note: Computer security incident handling. Publisher: NIST CSRC.

name: NIST SP 800-30 Rev 1 — Guide for Conducting Risk Assessments
note: Risk assessment methodology. Publisher: NIST CSRC.

name: NIST SP 800-115 — Technical Guide to InfoSec Testing
note: Security testing and assessment techniques. Publisher: NIST CSRC.

name: NIST SP 800-137 — Info Security Continuous Monitoring
note: ISCM for federal systems. Publisher: NIST CSRC.

name: NIST SP 800-218 — Secure Software Development Framework
note: SSDF practices for secure software. Publisher: NIST CSRC.

name: NIST Cybersecurity Framework (CSF) 2.0
note: The CSF 2.0 core reference. Publisher: NIST CSRC.

name: NIST CSRC Publications Library
note: Full searchable index of all NIST SP / FIPS / CSWP publications. Publisher: NIST CSRC.

name: DISA STIGs — Security Technical Implementation Guides
note: Public STIG library — configuration hardening guides for DoD systems. Publisher: DoD Cyber Exchange (DISA).

name: DISA STIG Downloads
note: Downloadable STIG packages (public release). Publisher: DoD Cyber Exchange (DISA).

name: SRG / STIG Tools (STIG Viewer)
note: STIG Viewer and supporting tools. Publisher: DoD Cyber Exchange (DISA).

name: DoD Cyber Exchange (Public)
note: DISA's public cyber resource hub — STIGs, SRGs, RMF, training. Publisher: DoD Cyber Exchange (DISA).

name: RMF Templates & Quick-Start Guides
note: Official RMF step quick-start guides and templates. Publisher: NIST CSRC.

name: DoD RMF Resources
note: DoD Risk Management Framework guidance and resources. Publisher: DoD / DISA.

name: Intelligence Community Directives (ICD series)
note: Official ICD series — IC policy directives and readouts. Publisher: ODNI.

name: IC on the Record — Public Releases
note: Declassified and public IC documents and readouts. Publisher: ODNI / intelligence.gov.

name: DoD Issuances (Directives / Instructions / Manuals)
note: Authoritative source for DoDD / DoDI / DoDM issuances. Publisher: Washington HQ Services (WHS).

name: Committee on National Security Systems (CNSS) Library
note: CNSS policies and instructions for national security systems. Publisher: CNSS.

name: DTIC — Defense Technical Information Center
note: Searchable DoD-funded technical reports and research (public release). Publisher: Defense Technical Information Center.

name: GAO — Defense Reports
note: Government Accountability Office reports on defense programs and weapon systems. Publisher: U.S. GAO.

name: CRS Reports
note: Nonpartisan congressional research, incl. weapon-system program reports. Publisher: Congressional Research Service.

name: CISA Resources & Tools
note: Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency guidance and advisories. Publisher: CISA.

name: Army University Press
note: Military review, doctrine analysis, and professional military education references. Publisher: Army University Press.

name: TC 3-22.9 — Rifle and Carbine (M4 / M16 series)
note: Public-release training circular: weapon function, optics, ammunition, and the marksmanship shot process for the M4/M4A1 carbine and M16 series. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA / TRADOC MCoE).

name: TC 3-23.35 — Pistol (M17 / M18 / M9)
note: Pistol marksmanship and employment — aiming, holsters/accessories, stability, control. Official APD record page with download. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA / TRADOC MCoE).

name: TC 3-23.30 — Grenades and Pyrotechnic Signals
note: Function, description, handling, and tactical employment of hand grenades and pyrotechnic signals/simulators. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA / TRADOC MCoE).

name: TC 3-22.50 — Heavy Machine Gun, M2 / M2A1 (.50 cal)
note: Operation, components, attachments, and gunnery for the M2 / M2A1 caliber .50 machine gun. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA / TRADOC MCoE).

name: TC 3-22.240 — Medium Machine Gun, M240 series
note: M240-series medium machine gun employment and gunnery. Official APD record page with download. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA / TRADOC MCoE).

name: TC 3-22.19 — MK19 40mm Grenade Machine Gun
note: Mechanical training, capabilities, and gunnery for the MK19 MOD 3 40mm grenade machine gun. Official APD record page. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA / TRADOC MCoE).

name: M249 Light Machine Gun — TBS Student Handout (B3M4138)
note: USMC Basic School student handout: characteristics, cycle of operation, and employment of the M249 SAW. Publisher: U.S. Marine Corps (TECOM / TBS).

name: ATP 3-21.8 — The Infantry Rifle Platoon and Squad
note: Doctrinal techniques for Infantry, Stryker, and Armored BCT platoons and squads — movement, offense, defense, patrolling. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA / TRADOC MCoE).

name: ATP 3-21.18 — Foot Marches
note: Planning and conduct of dismounted foot marches — load planning, rates, security, and march discipline. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA / TRADOC MCoE).

name: ATP 3-20.15 — Tank Platoon
note: TTPs for employment of the tank platoon (M1 Abrams) — formations, fire control, and combined-arms integration. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA / TRADOC) / USMC.

name: TC 3-09.81 — Field Artillery Manual Cannon Gunnery (FM 6-40)
note: Complete manual cannon gunnery problem — ballistics, firing charts, fire direction, and target attack. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA / Fires CoE).

name: TC 3-25.26 — Map Reading and Land Navigation
note: Armywide reference on map reading and land navigation — applies to every Soldier regardless of branch/MOS. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA / TRADOC MCoE).

name: TC 3-21.76 — Ranger Handbook
note: Small-unit tactics, battle drills, patrolling, and field craft reference. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA / Ranger Training Brigade).

name: FM 7-22 — Holistic Health and Fitness (H2F)
note: Army physical readiness training doctrine — programming, exercises, recovery, and injury prevention. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA / Center for Initial Military Training).

name: ATP 3-37.11 — CBRN Defense (Engineer/Maneuver Support)
note: Public-release CBRN defense techniques in support of maneuver. (Restricted ATP 3-11.32 deliberately excluded.). Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA / Maneuver Support CoE).

name: ATP 4-02.11 — Casualty Response, TCCC, and First Aid
note: Primary doctrinal guide for nonmedical Soldiers — care under fire, tactical field care, MARCH, hypothermia. Official MEDCoE release/announcement with link. Publisher: U.S. Army MEDCoE.

name: ATP 4-25.13 — Casualty Evacuation (CASEVAC)
note: Casualty movement, evacuation requests, and litter/non-litter carries. Publisher: HQDA (mirrored copy). Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA) — mirrored via GlobalSecurity.

name: TC 3-22.90 — Mortars (M224 60mm / M252 81mm / M120 120mm)
note: MOS 11C employment of the 60/81/120mm mortar systems — laying, fire commands, and gunnery. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA / TRADOC MCoE).

name: TC 3-22.10 — Sniper
note: Sniper equipment, ballistics, marksmanship fundamentals, field skills, and mission planning. Publisher: HQDA. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA) — mirrored via FAS.

name: FM 3-22.37 — Javelin Close Combat Missile System, Medium
note: Operation, employment, and gunner training for the Javelin anti-armor missile system. Publisher: HQDA. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA) — mirrored via FAS.

name: TC 3-25.150 — Combatives
note: Hand-to-hand and close-quarters combatives program — drills, techniques, and training progression. Publisher: HQDA. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA) — mirrored via FAS.

name: FM 3-97.61 — Military Mountaineering
note: Knots, anchors, rope systems, and movement techniques for mountainous terrain. Publisher: HQDA. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA) — mirrored via FAS.

name: ATP 3-90.97 — Mountain Warfare and Cold Weather Operations
note: Doctrine for operating in mountain and cold-weather environments — applies to all Soldiers. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA / Maneuver Support CoE).

name: FM 21-76 — Survival (SERE foundation)
note: Foundational survival doctrine — water, fire, shelter, food, navigation, all environments. Publisher: HQDA (public domain). Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA) — mirrored via FAS.

name: TC 3-21.5 — Drill and Ceremonies
note: Armywide standard for drill, manual of arms, and ceremonies. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA / TRADOC).

name: ATP 6-02.53 — Techniques for Tactical Radio Operations
note: Signal MOS reference: tactical radio nets, waveforms, SINCGARS/JCR planning, EW protection. Publisher: HQDA. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA) — mirrored via FAS.

name: ATP 3-04 — Army Aviation
note: Employment of Army aviation across the warfighting functions. Publisher: HQDA. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA) — mirrored via FAS.

name: ATP 3-06 — Urban Operations
note: Planning and conducting operations in dense urban terrain (ATP 3-06 / MCTP 12-10B). Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA / Combined Arms Center).

name: ATP 3-20.98 — Scout Platoon
note: Reconnaissance and security techniques for Cavalry scout platoons (ABCT/IBCT/SBCT). Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA / TRADOC).

name: FM 3-98 — Reconnaissance and Security Operations
note: Doctrine for Cavalry and recon/security organizations. Publisher: HQDA. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA) — mirrored via FAS.

name: ATP 3-01.81 — Counter-Unmanned Aircraft System (C-UAS) Operations
note: DEFENSIVE C-UAS — active/passive techniques and layered defense against UAS threats. Publisher: HQDA. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA) — mirrored via FAS.

name: FM 3-01 — U.S. Army Air and Missile Defense Operations
note: Air and missile defense doctrine — defensive integration across echelons. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA / Fires CoE).

name: FM 3-34 — Engineer Operations
note: Engineer Regiment keystone manual — combat, general, and geospatial engineering. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA / Maneuver Support CoE).

name: ADP 3-0 — Operations
note: Capstone doctrine: how the Army conducts unified land operations. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA / Combined Arms Center).

name: ADP 3-90 — Offense and Defense
note: Fundamental offensive and defensive tactics, control measures, and planning. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA / Combined Arms Center).

name: FM 3-90 — Tactics
note: Tactical concepts, formations, and the conduct of tactical operations. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA / Combined Arms Center).

name: FM 3-0 — Operations
note: The Army's keystone doctrine for prompt and sustained large-scale combat operations. Publisher: U.S. Army (HQDA / Combined Arms Center).
